stem: According to the chapter, what is the motivation behind compliance?

options.A: To avoid sanctions such as warnings, prohibition orders, penalties or imprisonment, out of fear of being caught
options.B: To go beyond the law because it is the right thing to do
options.C: To maximise the client's affection for the adviser
options.D: To increase the adviser's sales commissions

explanation: 教材指出合规的动机是逃避制裁(警告、训诫、禁止令、罚款、吊照、刑事监禁),核心是“被抓的恐惧”所带来的强制性。B 描述的是道德而非合规。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §2.1–2.2

stem: How does the chapter distinguish ethics from compliance?

options.A: Ethics is the letter of the law; compliance is the spirit of the law
options.B: Ethics reflects the spirit/intention of the law, while compliance refers to the letter of the law
options.C: Ethics and compliance are identical in practice
options.D: Compliance is always stricter than ethics

explanation: 教材明确:道德反映“法律的精神/意图”,合规仅是“法律的字面”。道德要求超越法律所规定的去做对的事,因此 A、D 颠倒或夸大,C 否定二者差异。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §2.4

stem: From which language does the word 'ethics' originate, and how did Aristotle describe the ethical person?

options.A: From Latin 'ethica'; the ethical person is one who maximises profit
options.B: From French 'éthique'; the ethical person is one who obeys all rules
options.C: From the Greek 'ethos'; the ethical person is one who has virtuous habits
options.D: From the Greek 'ethos'; the ethical person is one who avoids all decisions

explanation: 教材:ethics 源自希腊语 ethos;亚里士多德认为有德之人即拥有美德习惯(诚实、正义、荣誉、好脾气)之人,且美德可经教育、训练与实践习得。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §1.1

stem: In the Adam example, a seasoned trainer uses a training course to covertly recruit advisers from other firms without being transparent. What does the chapter conclude?

options.A: It breached compliance rules and was therefore unethical
options.B: It did not breach any compliance rules but was highly unethical
options.C: It was both compliant and ethical
options.D: It was non-compliant but perfectly ethical

explanation: Adam 的整个流程未违反任何合规规则,但因不透明地利用培训挖角,被教材判为“高度不道德”,正是“合规却不道德”的例证(合法未必合德)。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §2.5

stem: A representative signs up for a one-day CPD course, plays games at the back of the classroom, then signs out and collects the CPD hours. How should this be assessed?

options.A: Both compliant and ethical, since hours were earned
options.B: Technically/legally compliant in earning the hours, but ethically he has not done the right thing
options.C: Non-compliant, because he did not pay attention
options.D: Unethical and also a breach of compliance rules

explanation: 教材:他在法律/技术层面确已取得 CPD 学时(合规),但 CPD 的目的是持续提升知识与胜任力,他并未做对的事——道德上的答案多半为否。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §2.8

stem: The chapter calls the FA representative's role a 'privilege'. Why?

options.A: Because he can ask a complete stranger for sensitive financial information that people rarely share even with family
options.B: Because he earns the highest commissions in the economy
options.C: Because he is exempt from MAS regulation
options.D: Because he can sell products without giving any advice

explanation: 教材将顾问角色视为特权:他能向陌生人索取连挚友家人都未必透露的敏感财务信息;因此必须分析信息、推荐使客户处境优于从前的产品,不可轻视此特权。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §1.4

stem: Why does the chapter argue that ethics is especially critical in the financial services industry compared with selling tangible goods?

options.A: Because financial products are cheaper than cars or apartments
options.B: Because customers never need any advice for financial products
options.C: Because regulators forbid selling tangible goods
options.D: Because financial products are intangible, creating information asymmetry, so customers must rely on the representative for suitable advice

explanation: 教材:金融产品无形、存在信息不对称,客户无法像看汽车房产般评估,只能依赖代表给出适当建议,故信任与道德尤为关键。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §3.2–3.3, 3.11

stem: Which of the following correctly states Charles H. Green's trust equation as cited in the chapter?
explanation: 教材引用 Green 的信任方程式:Trust = (Credibility + Reliability + Intimacy) / Self-Interest。自利在分母,绝对以自利为先会摧毁前三要素,从而降低信任。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §3.21

stem: The chapter lists three motivations behind a representative's 'good' conduct toward clients. Which set is correct?
explanation: 教材:良好行为通常源于三种动机——(a) 自利、(b) 情感、(c) 道德。三者常同时存在,关键在何者为主导;D 是信任方程式要素,非动机。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §4.2

stem: Regarding self-interest, what is the chapter's key point?

options.A: Having self-interest is wrong and a professional must eliminate it entirely
options.B: Self-interest must always take priority over the client
options.C: There is nothing wrong with having self-interest, but the duty of care to the client must come first
options.D: Self-interest and affection are forbidden motivations

explanation: 教材:专业人士有自利并无不妥,但对客户的关怀义务(duty of care)必须先于自利。广义上以客户利益为先本身也符合自利。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §4.6

stem: The 2011 case where a representative sold fake policies to loyal ultra-HNW clients and pocketed $6.2m in premiums is used in the chapter to illustrate which motivation/factor?
explanation: 教材用此案例说明“合理化”:该代表自我合理化、说服自己无人会发现,从而为犯罪行为开脱。挪用客户资金还私债也属同类。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §4.11

stem: According to the report cited in the chapter, what were the MAS enforcement results over the 18 months to June that year?

options.A: 9 criminal convictions and $11.7 million in civil penalties
options.B: 25 criminal convictions and $3.4 million in civil penalties
options.C: 282 criminal convictions and $11.7 million in fines
options.D: 3 criminal convictions and $2 trillion in penalties

explanation: 教材引用《海峡时报》:MAS 在 18 个月内取得 9 项刑事定罪与 1,170 万元(S$11.7m)民事罚款。其余选项混淆了禁止令、监管提醒数目或财务罚款金额。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §3.17–3.18

stem: The quote 'It takes twenty years to build a reputation and five minutes to destroy it' is attributed in the chapter to whom, and what point does it support?

options.A: Ravi Menon; that compliance is enough
options.B: Peggy Pao; that enforcement is unnecessary
options.C: Aristotle; that virtue cannot be learned
options.D: Warren Buffett; that reputation built over years can be destroyed in minutes, so ethics matters

explanation: 教材将此名言归于巴菲特,用以说明声誉建立耗时而毁掉极快;社交媒体更使不当行为瞬间传播,故须守德护信。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §3.6, 3.8

stem: What is the chapter's concluding 'take-away' on the relationship between self-regulation and government supervision?

options.A: More regulation is always better regardless of conduct
options.B: If institutions and staff self-regulate and act ethically, there may be less need for stringent regulatory supervision
options.C: Ethics is irrelevant to the level of regulation
options.D: Only MAS, not firms, is responsible for ethical conduct

explanation: 教材结论:机构与员工若能自律守德,或可减少对金融业更严苛的监管与控制;反之,行业普遍缺德将招致更多政府监管。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.14 §3.3, 3.20
